motions.text: Are Social Networking Sites Good for Our Society?
Do Violent Video Games Contribute to Youth Violence?
Is Human Activity Primarily Responsible for Global Climate Change?
PE exams: A good idea? 你赞同体育测试达标和取得毕业证挂钩吗？
Kindness rewarded 拾金不昧将获奖励，你怎么看？

In recent times, it has become commonplace for people to “cancel (取消)” – block (屏蔽), delete (删除) or unfollow (取消关注) – each other on social media. It’s especially common when people are discussing political or social issues. To cancel or not?
Should we view nature as separate from people? 自然纪录片应该有选择地呈现更美好的局部吗？
You may have heard this saying many times: Not to pocket the property one picks up is a virtue ( 拾 金 不 昧 是 一 种 美 德 ). But how about offering incentives (奖励) for such good behavior?
Should young people write a will and testament?

Over the most recent Spring Festival, netizens have heatedly debated whether parents should be in charge of children’s lucky money for the new year. Do you think it’s a good idea that parents keep the money for their children?

Should parents tell children about their finances? Recently, a hot topic from popular internet show I Can I BiBi has sparked ( 引 发 ) a heated discussion online: Should parents tell their children that their family isn’t rich? Many people think we shouldn’t because we need to protect the kids’ self-esteem ( 自 尊 ). But others think it’s better to be honest. Do you think parents should tell their children that their family isn’t rich?

Recently, the topic of friendship has brought about a lot of discussion online. Some people think that aside from parental relationships, other relationships need to be deliberately ( 有 意 地 ) taken care of, too. Friendship is no exception. But others think that the most important thing for friends is to be comfortable and natural with one another. What do you think?

Many prehistoric creatures including dinosaurs and mammoths have long been extinct (灭绝的) on Earth, and the only way to see them again would be to revive ( 使 复 活 ) them. However, despite the advantages of bringing these animals back, there could be unintended consequences ( 后 果 ). Do you think scientists should revive these ancient creatures or keep them in the past?

Broadcasting classical music? During the novel coronavirus pandemic, many pianists and clarinetists ( 竖 笛 家 ) livestreamed ( 直 播 ) their music and held “cloud concerts”. Some people think that the live broadcast platform helps to spread interest in classical music. But others think that it doesn’t match with the elegant artistic seriousness of classical music. Do you support the livestreaming of classical music performance?

你支持学生暑假补课吗？School in summer? Recently, many students were faced with the reality of a shortened summer holiday after schools have re-opened and classes have resumed ( 恢 复 ). However, the Ministry of Education said that in principle, it is not recommended that students make up classes during summer vacation. Do you think that students should continue classes in the summer to make up for the lessons they have missed?

Should celebrities take responsibility for their fans? 你认为明星是否应该为粉丝的行为负责？ Recently, fans of Chinese actor and singer Xiao Zhan have caused great controversy (争议) on the internet. This brings an interesting topic to light: Should famous figures be accountable for the actions of his or her fans? Do you think that idols should be responsible for any misbehavior (不良 行为 ) on the part of those that follow them?

Would you want your test results to be widely known? 江苏省教育厅最新通知：小学初中不得公布学生考试成绩。你怎么 看？ Recently, the Jiangsu Provincial Department of Education released a document ( 文 件 ) that has brought heated discussion online. It stipulates (规定) that primary and junior high schools shouldn’t publish their students’ test results. Some students and their parents support it, but others think that students need to be clear about their rankings. Do you think students’ grades should be published?

Working from home every day？ 你支持永久在家办公吗? On May 22, Mark Zuckerberg, CEO of Facebook, said that half of the company could work from home within the next 5 to 10 years. Some people think that working from home allows for more flexibility (灵活 性), while others think that it will lead to people feeling more tired and working too much. Do you support the idea of working from home?

To be a nanny or not? 女硕士做保姆是人才浪费吗？ Recently, Liu Shuang from Zhejiang province became popular online. Graduated from Xi’an International Studies University with a master’s degree, she applied to be a nanny (保姆). Some people think that it is a waste for a person with such skills and education to become a nanny. But others think that it is a positive thing. Do you think it is fitting for those with this expertise (专业知识) to become nannies?

Memes: good or bad? “梗”能成为社交“快捷键”吗? Recently, China Youth Daily did a survey on memes ( 梗 ). They found that 67.34 percent of respondents (受调查者) will share new memes with their friends. Some people think that memes can create a relaxed atmosphere and a certain group identity (群体认同), but others think that internet buzzwords ( 热 词 ) should not be widely used in life nor embarrass people who don’t understand an inside joke. Do you think that memes can help social communication?

Textbook troubles 如何看教材分男女版？ Recently, the East China Normal University Press published a math textbook for junior high school students. The blue version was for boys, and the red version was for girls, which brought about a heated discussion online. Would you support the publishing of this kind of textbook?

Include PE in gaokao? 体育考试应该进高考吗？ Recently, some people have suggested that senior high school students should have a physical education (PE) exam as a part of their National College Entrance Examination. That way, students would pay more attention to PE. However, others think that it would be a burden (负担). Do you think PE should be included in the gaokao?

Should cameras be able to monitor students at home? 装摄像头监督孩子学习引热议，你觉得这算不算侵犯隐私？ Recently, a 14-year-old boy in Nanjing called the police, saying that his father wanted to install (安装) a camera to monitor (监视) him. It has brought about a heated online discussion. Do you support installing cameras at home?

Should PE include swimming or not? 游泳将被纳入中小学义务教育必修课，你对此怎么看？ Recently, the Ministry of Education said it would encourage primary and secondary schools to include swimming as a part of physical education (PE) classes. It has brought heated discussion. Some people think the measure is good for students, but others think swimming is not a necessary option. What do you think?

Is mailing a banner OK? 能不能寄送锦旗表示感激？ On Oct 29, Beijing Mentougou Blue Sky Rescue Team rescued 13 travelers trapped on a mountain. Days later, the rescue team received a silk banner (锦旗) of thanks by express mail from the saved travelers. Liu Tong, a Blue Sky member who participated in the rescue, said, “It’s an insult (侮辱).” However, some netizens think the mailed banner is an acceptable way to express thanks. Do you support the survivors showing thanks in this way?

Should you read classics? 高中生应该读名著吗？ In the 20th Senior Students Reading and Writing Competition in Jiangsu, there were discussions about reading literary classics. In many teachers’ views, students should read classics. However, many students have a different opinion. Do you support the idea that students should read classics?

Can you be too qualified to work as a nanny? 浙江杭州双语女硕士选择当保姆，你怎么看？ Recently, Liu Shuang from Zhejiang province became popular online. Graduated from Xi’an International Studies University with a master’s degree, she applied to be a nanny (保姆). Some people think that it is a waste for a person with such excellent skills to become a nanny. But others think that nannies should be highly educated. Do you support someone with such qualifications becoming a nanny?

Do you support the livestreaming of classical music? 疫情之下，古典音乐借助视频平台进行直播，你怎么看待这件事？ During the novel coronavirus pandemic, many pianists livestreamed their music and held “cloud concerts”. Some people think that the live broadcast platform helps to spread interest in classical music. However, others think that the social entertainment aspect of live broadcasting doesn’t match with the elegant (优雅的) artistic seriousness of classical music. Do you support it?

Should students make up classes during summer? 新冠疫情导致开学推迟。复课后，你支持学校占用暑假来补课吗？ After classes resumed, many students were faced with a shortened summer holiday. The Ministry of Education said that in principle, it is not recommended ( 建 议 ) that students make up classes during summer vacation. Do you think that students should continue classes in the summer to make up for the lessons they have missed?

What do you think about this perfectly scored essay? 2020 浙江满分作文惹争议，这个“满分”到底该不该给？ Recently, a composition ( 作 文 ) named Living on a Tree got a perfect score in the 2020 college entrance examination in Zhejiang. The professors praised the thinking behind the composition as “deep and stable”. However, many people thought the composition was “pretentious (做作的)”, believing that it used a lot of uncommon words, obscure ( 晦 涩 的 ) sentences and cited rare allusions ( 典 故 ). Do you think this kind of composition should get a high score or not?

Is monitoring kids OK? 你支持用摄像头监督孩子学习吗? Recently, a 14-year-old boy in Nanjing called the police, saying that his father wanted to install (安装) a camera to monitor (监控) him. It has brought about a heated online discussion. Do you support installing cameras at home?

Does Yang Chaoyue deserve Shanghai ‘hukou’? 杨超越落户上海，你怎么看？ Recently, the Shanghai Free-Trade Zone released a list of special talents who can get the Shanghai hukou, or household registration. Among them, Yang Chaoyue, a 22-year-old idol, has caused heated discussion online. Do you think she is eligible (合格的) for the Shanghai hukou?

How to deal with life? 你怎么看待 “糊弄学”？ Recently, a group of people who are “perfunctory ( 糊 弄 敷 衍 的 )” toward something, have been actively sharing amusing experiences on Douban. This so-called “perfunctorioloy ( 糊 弄 学 )” refers to the seemingly non-perfunctory way to deal with difficult things in life. Do you think it’s a good way to deal with difficult or awkward (尴尬的) situations?
Keep hair short? 你赞同学校规定学生的发型吗？
Can parents help exams? 你赞同家长替老师监考吗?

Encore: How open should campuses be to the public? In the height of winter, the Weiming Lake ice rink on the campus of Peking University is open to all skating enthusiasts. With spring, the cherry blossoms of Wuhan University attract millions of visitors both locally and from across the country. There are a lot of pros and cons for universities to open their campuses to the general public, and is there a right or wrong answer to the question: should university campuses stay open?
Leave books on our subways? 你支持“地铁丢书”活动吗?
Stop extra reading? 高中生禁止带课外书入校，你同意吗?
Books for boys ? 小学定制男生性别教材，你支持吗？
College campus or tourist spot?
Do homework online? 家庭作业电子化，是便捷还是负担？
Should employees lose weight? 你赞成公司设立“减肥奖”吗?
Is being asked to watch news OK? 单位要求员工看新闻，你支持吗?
Should animals be allowed to entertain people? 马戏团动物表演是否该叫停?
Should students be given a rank based on exam scores? 学生考试后应不应该排名?
No more handwriting in the future? 键盘能够代替手写吗?
Should paying extra money lead to better treatment?
Be responsible? 中学生晚十点没写完作业，家长签字可以免做，你同意吗？
Be punished for changing your mind? 高考录取后爽约，是失信还是一种重新选择?
Paying a charge just to read? 书店收费入场，你会去吗?
Deal or not deal? 律师妈妈的“协议教育”引热议。
Are uniforms with chips a good idea for students? “智能校服”引争议：是监控学生还是保护学生？
Should we have carriages for kids? 火车上设置“熊孩子车厢”，你支持吗?
Should people be banned from eating on subways? 你认为地铁上可以吃东西吗？
Are e-sports the real thing or a waste of students’ time? 电子竞技正式被国家统计局列为体育竞赛项目之一，你支持吗？
Are these signs too rude to help with traffic safety? “最直白”交通标语引热议。
New course controversy 家政专业是教学生当保姆吗？

Do students have a right to privacy in the classroom? 人脸识别技术进课堂，隐私保护边界在哪里？ A school in Sweden experimented with facial recognition to document ( 记 录 ) students’ attendance. The Swedish Data Protection Authority fined the school. Although the school claims it sought the consent (同意) of parents and students, the regulator still believed that students deserve ( 应 得 ) privacy in the classroom. Do you agree with the regulator?

Should boys and girls have separate classes in school? 学校该不该按性别来设置男女专属课？ Recently, in Chengdu, Sichuan, a primary school set exclusive (专属的) courses for boys and girls. They opened knitting (编织) classes to teach girls how to knit sweaters, and boys were shown how to make model airplanes, rockets and cars. Do you agree with this?

Should schools personalize homework assignments? 你认为学校应该推出“私人定制”作业吗？ Recently, a school in Hangzhou, Zhejiang gave out personalized homework assignments to students. Some people think this will help students study more efficiently ( 有 效 地 ). However, others think that students should have the same coursework and go through the same evaluations. Do you agree with having personalized homework?
Standard school hair? 学校该不该统一发型?

Do students agree with self-study sessions? 贵州大学下发通知，鼓励所有在校生进行晚自习。你认为大学生该 不该上晚自习？ Recently, students in Guizhou University were encouraged to attend evening self-study sessions (晚 自 习 ). According to the university, this initiative ( 新 方 案 ) is intended to help students develop a positive attitude about their studies. Do you agree with this announcement?

Do you want an immediate reward or not? 你希望自己的付出马上得到回报吗？ Recently, a survey conducted by the China Youth Daily showed that 68.2 percent of respondents (受访者) hoped that they would be rewarded immediately ( 立 刻 ) after making efforts. Though these individuals believe that there should be immediate rewards for making efforts, others think that this isn’t beneficial. Would you rather have an immediate reward or not?

Cats or paintings? 美术馆起火，你选择救猫还是救画? Recently, a hot debate topic from popular internet show U Can U Bibi (《奇葩说》) has taken the social media by storm: If the art museum was on fire, would you save a famous painting or a cat? Some people chose to save priceless paintings while others chose to save a living cat. Would you save cats instead of famous paintings?

Should a PE teacher become your head teacher? 对于杭州一位体育老师当班主任走红网络，你怎么看呢？ Recently, a physical education (PE) teacher in Hangzhou, Zhejiang, became a head teacher. It caused a heated discussion online. Some parents questioned the PE teacher’s knowledge of math or English. Other parents said the PE teacher could devote more time to the children because she has less academic (学科的) pressure. Would you agree with the PE teacher becoming your head teacher?

Should parents tell children that fairy tales are fake? 《奇葩说》辩题引热议：父母该不该告诉小朋友童话都是假的？ The hot debate topic from popular internet show I Can I BiBi (《奇葩说》) has become popular on social media Weibo: Should parents tell their children that fairy tales are fake? Some people think we shouldn’t. Fairy tales give kids hope and teach important life lessons. But others think that children need the ability to distinguish real from fake. Do you think parents should tell their children that fairy tales are fake?

Similar family interests? 孩子和家长要培养共同爱好吗？ Recently, China Youth Daily did a survey targeting people aged 18 to 35 to find out more about their life at home. As the result showed, many young people said they wanted to develop the same hobbies as their parents, while others believed that there was no need to do so. What do you think?

The benefit of being alone 独处有哪些利和弊? Recently, China Youth Daily did a survey on 2,001 people and found that half of them stayed home alone during the epidemic, and 66.5 percent of them felt that it was good to be alone. Some people think that those who are happiest in their own company are introverted (内向的) and lack self-confidence. Do you agree with the benefit of being alone?
Make days for issues? 你认为有必要设置各种纪念日吗？
A national costume? 你认为中国需要一套国服吗？
Changing classrooms 如果学校实行走班制，同学们按自己的课表去不同班级上课，你怎 么看？
Study our classics? 你赞同将中国传统文化列入高考范围吗？
Put new words in dictionary? 网络词语该不该入词典？
Cover schools with Wi-Fi? 中学校园全面覆盖无线网络，你同意吗？
Climb trees in class? 体育课“爬树”，你赞同吗？
Take foreign shows off? 你支不支持“限洋令”？
Online roommates? 你喜欢“私人订制” 趣味相投的室友吗?
Take classics from kids? “四大名著”适合儿童阅读吗？
Use internet at young age? 该不该让儿童过早接触网络？
Gossip ruins news? 明星绯闻过热是否影响公众关注其他新闻？
Too early for English? 现如今小学生学英语是否过早?
Classes for new parents? 家长经培训“持证上岗”，你支持吗?
Cool to use English? 中英文夹杂, 是“高大上”还是“硌牙”?
Textbooks: fact or fiction? 小学课文内容屡受争议，是用心良苦还是造假欺骗?
Changing majors? 大学放宽转专业限制，你支持吗?
Privacy important? 将教室监控当直播内容，你支持吗?
Choose money or passion? 准大学生选择专业，是兴趣优先还是就业重要？
Is it better to be wealthy? 寒门真的难出贵子吗？
Should mahjong become a Winter Olympic sport? 你支持麻将成为 2022 年北京冬奥会比赛项目吗?
College or scenic spot? 大学校园变 3A 级景区，你支持吗？
Can WeChat be a useful tool to help us study better? 微信群学习, 是真提升还是无用功?
Keep your eyes off your mobile phone 立法禁止过马路玩手机，你支持吗？
Martial arts novels OK to read? 《天龙八部》入选中学寒假推荐书目, 高中生要不要读武侠？
Do you trust self-driving cars? 自动驾驶路测国家规范出台，你信任无人车吗？
Poetry important? 学校招收诗词特长生，是广纳人才还是容忍偏科？
Should we be responsible for our parents’ actions? 父母失信影响子女入学，你支持吗？
Is it OK to have an idol? 父母不惧台风，陪孩子一起去追星，你赞成吗？
Would cosmetic surgery help students to find a job? 大学生整形再就业，颜值真的这么重要吗？
Should student military training be canceled? 学校是否应该取消军训?
Support new products? 老干妈跨界推卫衣，你买吗?
Would you buy notes from excellent students? 学霸笔记真的有用吗？
Should Mom and Dad really be showing us off online? 父母在社交网络上“晒娃”需要经过孩子同意吗？
Is filling a kid’s head with books such a bright idea? 五岁“神童”简历逆天，你羡慕吗?
Rescuing the Wall? 现代科技助力修复长城，你支持吗?
Is dress personal? 高校对学生着装出台规定，你怎么看？
Making us unsafe? 你会在学校使用一次性手机吗？

Too early for young children to learn their dialect? 方言进早教课堂能不能拯救上海话？ Recently, some Shanghai families have chosen to send their children to early-educational institutions where students are taught in Shanghainese, the Shanghai dialect ( 方 言 ). Most netizens expressed their support, but some netizens said learning Shanghainese would affect children’s learning Mandarin when they’re young. What do you think about this recent development?

Privacy in classrooms? 人脸识别技术侵犯隐私了吗？ A school in Sweden experimented with facial recognition software to record student attendance. The Swedish Data Protection Authority fined (罚款) the school. Although the school claims it sought the consent (同意) of parents and students, the regulator still believed that students deserve ( 应 得 ) privacy (隐私) in the classroom. Do you agree with the regulator?

Do you support using clone technology for our pets? 花 38 万让宠物“回来”, 你愿意吗？ Recently, a woman from Hangzhou, Zhejiang province, spent 380,000 yuan to clone her deceased ( 已 故 的 ) pet dog. It caused a lot of discussion online. Some people think it’s worthwhile because they have fond (温情的) memories of their pets and they don’t want to lose their furry friends forever. But others think that while we can clone an animal’s flesh, its soul cannot be copied. Do you agree with the woman?

Open or closed? 中国大学该不该建围墙? Recently, Xi’an Jiaotong University brought about a heated discussion online after its new campus decided to have no perimeter walls (围墙). Some people think it’s unsafe, while the president of the university believes that the open campus is a demonstration of inclusiveness (包 容). Do you agree with this idea?
Replace our chopsticks? 为了环保，应该弃用筷子，改用刀叉吗？

Are degrees absolutely necessary for employment? Some corporate leaders in the US say that millions of jobs requiring a four-year college degree can be done WITHOUT that level of education. They hope lowering education qualification requirements can address inequalities in business and society. Are degrees absolutely necessary for employment? / Fast food loyalty programs have grown during pandemic. / Is it time to talk about "responsible" global travels?

Are food delivery apps charging too much commission? A leading food delivery app made nearly 59 billion Yuan (or 9 billion USD) in commission from food delivery alone in 2020. It's one indicator of the prosperous business which saw a surge during the coronavirus outbreak. Are platforms charging too much commission?
Should independent women receive betrothal gifts? If a woman identifies herself as an independent woman, is she allowed to receive betrothal money?

Is English still an obsession for China's tiger parents? In major Chinese cities, some parents are taking drastic measures to secure their kids a spot to sit massively oversubscribed English language tests. Why it is so important to them?

Can a paternity leave help boost birth rate? Shanghai Women's Federation will propose a mandatory paternal leave, among many other measures, to promote gender equality and support people's decision to have bigger families.

Chinese universities release salary of fresh graduates A small-scale survey about graduate income from Nanjing University claimed public attention. Is it true that their graduates on average are earning 178,000 yuan annually?

Some young couples are choosing to live with their respective families, rather than move in together after marriage. Is this still marriage? 119Is over-educated a problem? A new study shows that about half of online job-seekers in China are at least two years overeducated. And it has an effect on people's salary and the labor market as a whole.
Some high schools in Beijing have begun offering career planning courses. Is it too early to talk about career planning?
On microblog Weibo, the hashtag "Parents win law suit over 22-year-old daughter who refuses to raise her 2-year-old brother" has attracted 600 million reads and 66 thousand comments as of date.
The student accommodation policy of one university has triggered a heated debate over the affordability and equality of education in China. Should students rent accommodation outside of campus?

Should parents get a refund on private schools fees? Parents clamor for tuition refunds at private schools in China, as coronavirus shutters campuses. Should schools and universities Is it time to abandon the overwork culture?
Is it time to raise the retirement age?
Should English be removed from school curriculum?
Should parents accompany their children to college?
Is the cashless era coming?
Is it time to change the communal eating culture?
Should divorce have a mandatory cooling-off period?
Should the law protect underage criminals?
Should euthanasia be allowed?
Should employees take naps at work?
Should theme parks allow outside food ？
Overseas study tours: An eye-opening experience or waste of money?
Should universities have a buddy program for intl students?
Should museums be free?
Should using phones when crossing the road be fined?
Should spanking be banned?
Should there be a ban on gifts to teachers?
Should no-homework policy be encouraged?
Should elder sister raise her little sibling?
Should animals be kept in captivity?

Pro: China’s “Four Great Classic Novels” are suitable for children. 中国“四大名著”适合儿童阅读。 Con: China’s “Four Great Classic Novels” are not suitable for children. 中国“四大名著”不适合儿童阅读。 Notes(背景提示，仅供参考): China’s “Four Great Classic Novels” have played an important part in Chinese literature. Students are asked to read them from childhood. However, nowadays some teachers think that these novels are not suitable for children to read, saying that Outlaws of the Marsh (《水浒传》) is about robbing (抢劫) rich people and Journey to the West is too influenced by Buddhism (佛教). What’s your opinion?

Pro(正 方): Students while choosing their college majors should give priority to their passion instead of the possibility of finding a good job in the future. 学生选择大学专业时应该优先考虑兴趣。 Con( 反 方 ): Students while choosing their college majors should give priority to the possibility of finding a good job in the future instead of their passion. 反方：学生选择大学专业时应该优先考虑将来能否找到好工作。 Notes(背景提示，仅供参考): Many high school graduates are starting a new journey on college campuses as freshmen. In terms of choosing their college majors ( 专 业 ), some students are concerned ( 关 心 的 ) about coming out with a good job, while others care more about studying something that really interests them. Which factor do you value more?

Pro: Museums should charge admission fee. 参观博物馆应该收费。 Con: Museums should not charge admission fee. 参观博物馆不应该收费。 Notes(背景提示，仅供参考): International Museum Day falls on May 18. The debate over whether museums should be free is a big one right now. It's the source of a lot of discussion in the popular press and nonprofit boardrooms alike. Should museums be free? What is your opinion?

Pro: Textbooks can include fake stories. 课本可以含有虚假故事。 Con: Textbooks should not include fake stories. 课本不可以含有虚假故事。 Notes(背景提示，仅供参考): We are all familiar with this story: Thomas Edison, as a boy of seven, put mirrors near oil lamps to light up the room during his mother’s appendectomy (阑尾切除术). It has been in Chinese primary school textbooks since the 1980s, but recently many people pointed out that it might not be true. The world’s first appendectomy is believed to have taken place in 1886, and it was 1854 when Edison was 7. Can textbooks include fake stories?

Pro: Robot pets can replace real ones. 机器人宠物可以代替真宠物。 Con: Robot pets cannot replace real ones. 机器人宠物不可以代替真宠物。 Notes(背景提示，仅供参考): Recently, a New Zealand company has developed robotic dolphins that look exactly like the real animals. An aquarium introduced these animatronic (电子动 物的) dolphins as an alternative of real ones. Would you support it or not?

Pro: Primary schools should provide courses for Shanghai dialect learning in order to protect the dialect. 正方：为保护上海方言，小学应该开设上海话课程。 Con: Primary schools do not need to provide courses for Shanghai dialect learning in order to protect the dialect. 反方：为保护上海方言，小学不需要开设上海话课程。 Notes(背景提示，仅供参考): Recently, some Shanghai families have chosen to send their children to early-educational institutions where students are taught in Shanghainese, the Shanghai dialect (方言). Most netizens expressed their support, but some netizens said learning Shanghainese would affect children’s learning Mandarin when they’re young. What do you think about this recent development?

Pro: Spanking children should be allowed. 体罚孩子应该被允许。 Con: Spanking children should not be banned. 体罚孩子不应该被允许。 Notes(背景提示，仅供参考): The French parliament voted to outlaw "corporal punishment or humiliation" during a session last Friday morning. If the bill is adopted, France will become the 55th country in the world to ban spanking children. The news was the most searched topic on Sina Weibo, the Chinese Twitter-like platform. Spanking, also called corporal punishment, is a regular discipline method for parents to put an immediate stop to bad behavior. But is it okay to spank?

Pro: Money can buy opportunity of skipping lines. 正方：多花钱就可以不排队。 Con: Money cannot buy opportunity of skipping lines. 反方：多花钱也不可以不排队。 Notes( 背 景 提 示 ， 仅 供 参 考 ): Shanghai Disney Resort has created discussions on Chinese social media because of its VIP service, which allows high-paying customers to skip lines ( 插 队 ) for rides and attractions. Do you support this service?
Students from wealthier families tend to Is it better to be wealthy? Students from poor families tend to stand less chance to 寒门真的难出贵子吗？
Should people be banned from eating on subways? 你认为地铁上可以吃东西吗？
Should firecrackers( 鞭 炮 ) should be allowed during the Spring Festival? 春节应不应该放鞭炮？
Woman teachers are more popular than man teachers in primary school.
Being a leader in the class does good to one’s study.
Should Mom and Dad really be showing us off online? 父母在社交网络上“晒娃”需要经过孩子同意吗？
Should students be given a rank based on exam scores? 学生考试后应不应该排名?
Ipads should be promoted（推广）as a teaching aid.
Corporal punishment（体罚） should be used in primary schools.

Should parents tell children that fairy tales are fake? 《奇葩说》辩题引热议：父母该不该告诉小朋友童话都是假的？ The hot debate topic from popular internet show I Can I BiBi (《奇葩说》) has become popular on social media Weibo: Should parents tell their children that fairy tales are fake? Some people think we shouldn’t. Fairy tales give kids hope and teach important life lessons. But others think that children need the ability to distinguish real from fake. Do you think parents should tell their children that fairy tales are fake?
Martial arts novels OK to read? 《天龙八部》入选中学寒假推荐书目, 高中生要不要读武侠？
Cool to use English? 中英文夹杂, 是“高大上”还是“硌牙”?
Take classics from kids? “四大名著”适合儿童阅读吗？

Too early for young children to learn their dialect? 方言进早教课堂能不能拯救上海话？ Recently, some Shanghai families have chosen to send their children to early-educational institutions where students are taught in Shanghainese, the Shanghai dialect ( 方 言 ). Most netizens expressed their support, but some netizens said learning Shanghainese would affect children’s learning Mandarin when they’re young. What do you think about this recent development?
、Paying a charge just to read? 书店收费入场，你会去吗?
Do homework online? 家庭作业电子化，是便捷还是负担？

Should Libraries Charge Late Fees? Have you ever been fined for returning a book to the library after its due date? It might seem reasonable for libraries to charge late fees. How else would they get anyone to return their books? But the practice could be on its way out, at least for public libraries. Many of them have banned late fees because these fees can discourage people from using the library. And the whole purpose of a library is to make books available. So we asked our readers what they think about late fees. Keep them? Or get rid of them? Here’s what some had to say.

Should Perfect Attendance Be Rewarded? It’s common for schools to reward students for achievements in sports and academics. Some schools reward kindness. What about perfect attendance? Should students be rewarded for showing up every day? There’s no doubt that success at school depends on being present and ready to learn. But some people say rewarding perfect attendance isn’t a fair practice. It penalizes kids who miss school for reasons beyond their control. We put the question to our readers. Here’s what some of them had to say.

Should Kids Use AI to Do Homework? Artificial intelligence is part of our everyday lives. It makes our smart devices work, and you may already be using an AI program in school. But advanced chatbots, like ChatGPT, are new. They are called generative AI. Ask a chatbot a question, and it will generate a whole essay. Some educators worry that chatbots could prevent students from learning on their own. Should students use AI to do their school assignments? We asked our TFK Kid Reporters to argue both sides of the case.
Should tracking devices be allowed on student IDs?
That single-sex schools should be banned.
That school grades should be grouped based on ability rather than age.
That stay-at-home parents should be paid a wage by the government.
That parents should be held responsible for the crimes of their children.
Which is more fun: Summer or Winter? （XA）
Is it better to be the youngest or the oldest sibling? （XA）
Cats vs. Dogs: Which is the better pet? （XA）
Should birthday presents be a surprise or chosen by the birthday person? （XA）
Should school be year-round with more breaks throughout?
Should school hours be shortened?

Is it okay to keep secrets from parents? 183. Are smartwatches hijacking our kids' attention? While some parents view smartwatches as a safer alternative to smartphones for kids, offering location tracking without the additional risks of full-fledged devices, there's a catch – these watches can become so addictive that children struggle to put them down, even during mealtimes or when it's time for bed. So let's talk about it: are smartwatches for kids a convenience or cause for concern?

Chinese dialect products: The more local, the more international We're diving into the rich and diverse world of Chinese dialects. As emerging writers, musicians, and filmmakers embrace local dialects, they discover new voices and unique ways to express their creativity. We'll be discussing the significance of dialects in modern Chinese literature, music and film, and why preserving these linguistic treasures is crucial for cultural diversity. 185. Encore: How open should campuses be to the public? Dear listeners, get ready for an encore as we revisit some of our favorite episodes from the year! In the height of winter, the Weiming Lake ice rink on the campus of Peking University is open to all skating enthusiasts. With spring, the cherry blossoms of Wuhan University attract millions of visitors both locally and from across the country. There are a lot of pros and cons for universities to open their campuses to the general public, and is there a right or wrong answer to the question: should university campuses stay open? 186. Should grass be walked on? To tread or not to tread? While some Chinese parks enforce strict 'keep off the grass' policy, others embrace the idea of open invitations. We discuss the changing landscape of recreational norms in green spaces. 187. Is AI amplifying bias or addressing it in job recruitment? We discuss the role of AI-enabled hiring in the recruitment process around the world! AI, like humans, is susceptible to biases, underscoring the importance of vigilant oversight. Together, let's navigate the intricacies of AI hiring bias, and contemplate more equitable recruitment outcomes. 188.Is memorization still important in the age of AI? （在 AI 时代，记忆背诵仍然重要吗？） 189.Will AI teachers replace human teachers in the future? （未来 AI 教师会取代人类教师吗？） 190.Students who excel in mathematics are inherently more intelligent than those who excel in language arts.(数学成绩优异的学生天生比语 文成绩优异的学生更聪明。)
Schools should prioritize mathematical education over language arts, as it develops sharper minds. (学校应优先发展数学教育而非语 文教育，因为数学更能培养敏锐的思维。)
High math achievers have higher IQs than high language achievers, proving they are smarter." (数学成绩优异者比语文成绩优异者智商更 高，证明他们更聪明。
Should Robots Be Designed with Human-like Faces?*（机器人是 否应该被设计成具有类人面孔？）
Is It Necessary for Robots to Have Human Facial Features?（机器 人是否需要有人的面部特征？）
Do the Benefits of Human-like Faces on Robots Outweigh the Drawbacks?（机器人拥有人脸的好处是否大于弊端？）
Humanoid Faces in Robots: Helpful or Harmful ?（人形脸机器人： 有益还是有害？）
Should AI Robots Avoid Human-like Facial Designs to Prevent Ethical Issues?（为避免伦理问题，AI 机器人是否应避免类人面部设 计？）
Are Human-faced Robots More Effective in Social Interactions?（拥 有人脸的机器人在社交互动中是否更有效？）
Is it more important to learn English or coding?（学英语和学编程 哪个更重要？）
Does Giving Robots Human Faces Enhance or Diminish User Trust? （赋予机器人人脸会增强还是削弱用户信任？）
Many young people today put self-care first ？年轻人“没福硬享” 的生活态度，你支持吗？
Is AI the better homework helper?
AI 与家长，谁更适合辅导作业？ Unlike parents who may get angry sometimes, AI can help with homework and always stay calm. Do you think AI is a better teacher than parents?
Live now or save later?

There should be no grades in elementary school, only “OK” or “fail”.
It is better to be an adult than a child
Children should be allowed to choose their own bedtime
Should kids be allowed to have a say in family decisions?
Should kids have a say in what they learn in school?
Should kids have a say in what they learn in school?
Pro: Birthday presents should be a surprise. Con: Birthday presents should be chosen by the birthday person.
时尚租赁作为新兴模式，正在重新定义人们对时尚的消费观念。 过去，人们为了跟上潮流，不得不购买大量服饰，但现如今，租赁 时尚已成为更加普遍的选择。 Pro: It is better to rent clothes than buy them. Con: It is better to buy clothes than rent them.

目前我国很多对书的内容进行 二次加工的“替人读书“平台”， 用几分钟到几十分钟不等的时间，高度浓缩一本图书的精华，为想 读书的人节省时间，质疑者认为“替人读书”平台是功利性阅读的 产物，它只会让人越来越懒于读一本完整的书 Pro: It is good for people to read the original book. Con: It is good for people to read what is chewed and digested by others.
Pro: If technology allows, products should be made to last longer. Con: Even if technology allows, products should not be made to last longer.
Pro: Summer is better than winter. Con: Winter is better than summer.
Pro: The cat is a better pet than the dog. Con: The dog is a better pet than the cat.
Pro: Tourists are causing more good than harm. Con: Tourist are causing more harm than good.
Pro: It is better to rent clothes than buy them. Con: It is better to buy clothes than rent them.
Pro: There should be a limit on wealth. Con: There should not be a limit on wealth.
Pro: Guardians(监护人) should be responsible for minor crimes(未 成年人犯罪). Con: Guardians should not be responsible for minor crimes.
Pro: Birthday presents should be a surprise. Con: Birthday presents should be chosen by the birthday person.
Pro: Exams are the best way to test pupils. Con: Exams are not the best way to test pupils.
Pro: Historical films need to be accurate. Con: Historical films do not need to be accurate.
Pro: It is good for people to read the original book. Con: It is good for people to read what is chewed and digested by others.
Should kids have a say in what they learn in school?

Following the opening of China's first lounge for pets waiting to board on flights at the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport on May 8 in South China's Guangdong Province, passengers have engaged in discussions about whether pets could also board high-speed railways. Agree or Disagree?

在传统的教育理念中，“儿不打不成器”、“严父出孝子”等这 样的理论备受推崇，而当下更加开明的教育理念们则更倾向于释放 孩子的天赋潜能，让孩子拥有快乐、自由的成长空间。而当这两种 教育理念发生碰撞时，家长们难免会在“严点”好还是“松点”好 教育方式上犹豫不定。 Here is the question: Is it better for parents to be tolerant or strict？ Pro: It is better for parents to be tolerant(宽容的). Con: It is better for parents to be strict(严厉的).

在日常生活中常常会发生这样的情况：父母常常会搬出别人家 的孩子来跟自己的孩子做比较，很大一部分的原因是希望能够帮助 到自己的孩子——在孩子不好好学习时，给孩子找一个对手；在孩 子太得意时，给孩子一个提醒。那么问题来了：父母经常提“别人 家的孩子”有多好，这对孩子来说是激励还是打击？ Here is the question: To mention or not to mention the merits of other kids? Is it a motivation or humiliation? Pro: It is a motivation( 动 力 ) for kids if the parents mention the merits(优点) of other kids. Con: It is a humiliation(羞辱) for kids if the parents mention the merits of other kids.

近年来，北京、上海、广州、深圳这四座中国内地一线城市的 人口聚集及由此引发的“大城市病”问题引发学界持续讨论。主流意 见认为，政府不应控制大城市的人口规模，应当让人口在城乡和城 市间自由流动，以实现资源的最优配置。但由此引发的社会问题也 层出不穷，包括养老问题。对此，你怎么看？ Here is the question: Should the size of the mega-city be limited? Pro: The size of the mega-city should be limited. Con: The size of the mega-city should not be limited.

全国人大代表提出建议取消中考并将高中阶段纳入到义务教育 阶段中，因为很多青少年可能在初中阶段心智发展并不成熟，还没 有意识到学习的重要性，在这时候就将学生进行分流，对很多学生 的发展都非常不利。北京大学国家发展研究院教授、中国经济研究 中心主任姚洋曾强调，现在只要上了普高，几乎就能上大学，只不 过是好坏问题。而中考是决定能上普高还是职高，决定孩子一辈子， 所以大家现在卷中考。这么做的结果是，压力往下传导了，是倒着 走。所以呼吁取消中考，目的是要让孩子们都上完高中，将人才的 选拔交给高考，那时再决定去学技术还是上大学。 Here is the question: Is it the boon or bane to abolish Zhongkao? Pro: I support abolishment of Zhongkao. Con: I oppose abolishment of Zhongkao.

全职父母：全国政协委员、广西壮族自治区柳州市人民检察院 副检察长韦震玲提交提案，建议建立家庭全职服务成员职业权利保 障制度，维护家庭全职服务成员的合法权益。她认为，因特殊事由、 特殊时期（3 岁以下哺乳幼龄教育期，照顾失能父母、配偶等）辞 职回归家庭承担家庭全职服务的成员，可享受工龄累计及劳动社会 保障等相关待遇；夫妻双方如果有一方选择回归家庭为家庭提供全 职服务，可以适当减免从业配偶个人所得税等。 Here is the question: Is it appropriate(合适的) for stay-at-home parents to be paid a subsidiary(补贴)? Pro: It is appropriate for stay-at-home parents to be paid a subsidiary. Con: It is inappropriate for stay-at-home parents to be paid a subsidiary.

艺术创作有一个很经典的说法“源于生活高于生活”。电影作 为一种艺术表现形式，它不是为了记录真实的历史而存在的，它所 表达的是编剧及导演想要让观众看到的属于他们个人的思想，这思 想也许来源于历史，也许来源生活，也许纯属个人创作，对于在这 个表达过程中出现的“不符合历史真相”的情节，是需要包容的，电 影就是电影；也有人说，对于杜撰情节的包容是有底线的，历史上 的重大事件不容歪曲，正确的价值观不容扭曲，这一点必须严格遵 守。那么问题来了：历史电影必须准确吗？ Here is the question: Do historical films need to be accurate? Pro: Historical films must be accurate. Con: Historical films do not have to be accurate.

近来，关于高铁车厢内是否可以携带宠物引起了热议。一些人 认为，宠物是人类的朋友，只要卫生手续齐全，拴上链子，就可以 随时带在身边，哪怕是上高铁，坐在主人身旁的座位上，主人为宠 物买票就行；也有些人认为，动物的行为不可控，且身上常散发异 味，带入高铁车厢会影响其他乘客的乘车感受。你觉得宠物可以带 入高铁乘客车厢吗？ Here is the question: Is it appropriate( 合 适 的 ) to carry pets with the passengers on the high-speed train? Pro: It is appropriate to carry pets with the passengers on the high-speed train. Con: It is inappropriate to carry pets with the passengers on the high-speed train.

学校中的年级，是指学生按照年龄、学业水平和学习能力的不 同划分的分类制度。随着教育理念的不断升级，年级教育也随之转 变观念。除了对学生进行统一管理与教育外，还需注重个性化、多 样性发展，尊重不同学生的特点和需求，让每一个学生得到全面的 发展，提高学生的综合素质。那么问题来了：学校的年级必须按照 年龄来划分，还是可以按照能力划分？ Here is the question: Should students be grouped based on age or ability? Pro: Students should be grouped based on their ages. Con: Students should be grouped based on their abilities.

有人提出，学生的暑假时间太长，有浪费时间之嫌，把暑假时 间分散成几个小长假是不是会更高效地进行修整，对此你怎么看？ Here is the question: Is it better to have a summer vacation or multiple(多个) shorter vacations throughout(遍布) the year? Pro: It is better to have a long summer vacation. Con: It is better to have multiple shorter vacations.

为了迎合社会的要求给学生减负，学校的课程内容减之又减， 很多学校到中午以后就没什么课可以上了，于是要么自习，要么放 学，有些学校甚至两点半就放学了。那么问题来了：那么早放学， 真的好吗？ Here is the question: Is it good to finish a school day early in the afternoon? Pro: It is good to finish a school day early in the afternoon. Con: It is good to finish a school day late in the afternoon.

Welcome, dear class, to "AI 101: How Robots Are Invading Our Classrooms!" Don't worry, they're not here to take over - not yet, at least. Today, we will be exploring the exciting and sometimes controversial world of artificial intelligence in our schools. Will AI be the ultimate study buddy or the class troublemaker?

Recently, a student who ranked first on the postgraduate (研究生) entrance exam at Nanjing University failed the admission interview. It’s likely because he was reported by internet users for recording videos of himself abusing ( 虐 待 ) a cat in his dormitory ( 宿 舍 ). Should such behavior impact an individual’s admission to higher education?

Parents are now stepping into classrooms in elementary schools in Beijing and Shanghai, to participate in a new cooperative education model. While it aims to foster stronger bonds between home, school, and community, it also raises questions: Can this kind of parental involvement transform education for the better? Is it fair to expect such a high level of involvement from parents?
Are gifted and talented programs in schools more harmful than beneficial due to their exclusionary nature?
Should parents be allowed to post pictures of their children on social media without their consent?
Are spanking and other forms of physical punishment an effective method of discipline?
Is Homework Beneficial(good)?
Should Zoos Exist?
Natural talent is more important for success than hard work.
It is better to be well-versed in multiple topics than a specialis

Pro: Spanking and other forms of physical punishment are an effective method of discipline. 正方：打屁股等形式的体罚是有效的训诫方式。 Con: Spanking and other forms of physical punishment are not an effective method of discipline. 反方：打屁股等形式的体罚不是有效的训诫方式。
Pro: Zoos should exist. 正方：动物园应该存在。 Con: Zoos should not exist. 反方：动物园不应该存在。

Pro: Parents Should be allowed to post pictures of their children on social media without the children’s consent. 正方：把孩子的照片放到社交媒体上，家长不需要经过孩子同意。 Con: Parents Should not be allowed to post pictures of their children on social media without the children’s consent. 反方：把孩子的照片放到社交媒体上，家长需要经过孩子同意。

近日，《关于进一步严格落实义务教育“双减”工作有关规定的 通知》指出，严格落实作业总量有关要求，小学一二年级不布置书 面家庭作业，但是一些家长认为书面家庭作业可以帮助学生巩固和 回顾在课堂上学到的知识。 Pro: Homework does more good than harm. 正方：家庭作业利大于弊。 Con: Homework does more harm than good. 反方：家庭作业弊大于利。

Background: Parents are now stepping into classrooms in elementary schools in Beijing and Shanghai, to participate in a new cooperative education model. While it aims to foster stronger bonds between home, school, and community, it also raises questions: Can this kind of parental involvement transform education for the better? Is it fair to expect such a high level of involvement from parents? 背景：在北京和上海，家长们走进小学课堂，参与到新的合作教育 模式中。这样做的目的是为了更好地把家庭、学校和社区紧密团结 在一起。那么问题来了：家长如此的参与模式会让教育变得更好吗？ 要求家长们有如此高的参与度，对家长来说公平吗？ Pro: It is a good move to have such a high level of parental involvement in education. 正方：让家长走进小学课堂这种高度参与教育是一种好的举措。 Con: It is not a good move to have such a high level of parental involvement in education. 反方：让家长走进小学课堂这种高度参与教育不是一种好的举措。
Natural talent is more important for success than hard work.

ChatGPT 和 Sora 刚问世便火遍全球，反映了人工智能的巨大影 响力和无尽潜力，未来似乎有无限可能。但是人工智能进入我们的 教室后也有人提出担忧很多问题都能通过人工智能找到答案了，学 生会不会因此不再进行思考、不记忆，不学习了？ Pro: AI will be the ultimate study buddy. 正方：人工智能会成为终极学习伙伴。 Con: AI will be the class troublemaker. 反方：人工智能会成为课堂麻烦者。

Background: Recently, a student who ranked first on the postgraduate (研究生) entrance exam at Nanjing University failed the admission interview. It’s likely because he was reported by internet users for recording videos of himself abusing ( 虐 待 ) a cat in his dormitory (宿舍). Here’s the question: Should such behavior impact an individual’s admission to higher education? 背景：最近，南京大学研究生入学考试第一名在面试中被拒了，很 有可能是因为有网友爆料他在宿舍虐猫。那么问题来了：类似虐猫 这种行为应不应该影响高校录取？ Pro: Such behavior should impact an individual’s admission to higher education. 正方：这种“虐猫”行为应该影响到个人接受高等教育的机会。 Con: Such behavior should not impact an individual’s admission to higher education. 反方：这种“虐猫”行为不应该影响到个人接受高等教育的机会。

背景：三字经上讲“养不教，父之过”，强调了爸爸妈妈在子女 教育中的责任，指出养育子女不仅仅是提供物质上的满足，更重要 的是要进行品德和知识的教育。 Pro: It is parents’ fault(错) if the children are not educated well. Con: It is not parents’ fault if the children are not educated well.
背景：有人认为，我们做事情需要“尽力而为”，但也有人认为 需要“量力而行”，这种看似矛盾的观点引起了人们的讨论。 Pro: We should do our best in everything we do. Con: We should act within our capabilities(能力).

背景：现在很多家长在假期外出游玩时让孩子把作业带上，适 当的作业可以让孩子保持学习状态，也有人认为占用旅游时间，降 低了旅游放松的效果。这一做法引起了人们的热议。 Pro: Students should bring homework on vacation tours. Con: Students should focus(聚焦，集中) solely(仅仅) on enjoying their vacation tours.
背景：我们鼓励别人时，常会说“永不言弃”；但也有人说，学 会放弃是一种智慧。放弃是软弱还是智慧成为了一个讨论焦点。 Pro: It is better to never give up. Con: Knowing when to quit is a wiser choice.
背景：人们常认为“一分耕耘，一分收获”，辛苦劳动必有回报。 然而，现实中也存在许多努力后仍未能如愿以偿的情况。辛苦劳动 是否一定有所得引起了广泛的讨论。 Pro: Hardwork always pays off(回报). Con: Hardwork does not always pay off.
背景：随着 AI 技术发展，AI 辅导工具因个性化、高效等优势 被广泛应用。父母辅导作业则能提供情感支持和价值观引导。关于 哪种方式更适合辅导作业，引起了广泛探讨。 Pro: AI is the better homework helper. Con: Parents are the better homework helper.
背景：在学习和生活中，反馈起着至关重要的作用。然而，关 于正面反馈和负面反馈哪个更重要，一直存在争议。 Pro: Positive feedback outweighs negative feedback. Con: Negative feedback outweighs positive feedback.

背景：在个人成长的道路上，人们常常思考自己的身份和目标。 是明确“我想成为怎样的人”更重要，还是“我不想成为怎样的人”更 具指导意义？这一问题引发了广泛的讨论。 Pro: "Who I want to be" is more important than "Who I don't want to be." Con: "Who I don't want to be" is more important than "Who I want to be."

背景：“宁为玉碎不为瓦全”出自《北齐书·元景安传》。意思为 宁愿做玉器被打碎，也不做瓦器而得到保全。比喻宁愿为正义事业 牺牲，不愿丧失气节，苟且偷生。但在现实生活中，是否应始终坚 持这一原则引发了广泛讨论。 Pro: Better to be a broken jade than an intact(原封未动的) tile(瓦片). Con: Better to be an intact tile than a broken jade.

背景：在传统文化的传承浪潮中，诗词朗读的发音选择成为焦 点（比如：“远上寒山石径斜”中的“斜”传统吟诵念 xiá，现代教 学普遍采用标准读音 xié）。现代普通话的普及与古音的文化价值相 互碰撞，引发了关于如何更好地传承诗词文化的热烈讨论。 Pro: The recitation(吟诵) of classical poetry should primarily follow the pronunciation of modern Mandarin. Con: The recitation of classical poetry should primarily follow the ancient phonetics(语音).
背景：在人生旅途中，人们常常思考命运是由自己掌控还是由 天意决定。这一古老而深刻的命题引发了关于自由意志与宿命的无 尽探讨。 Pro: My fate is in my own hands. Con: My fate is in the hands of destiny.
背景：随着在线教育的兴起，越来越多的明星开设网络课程（比 如吴彦祖开英语课程），引发了公众对其教育价值和商业宣传的讨 论。 Pro: Celebrity(名人) online classes are more about publicity(宣传). Con: Celebrity online classes offer genuine(真正的) education.
背景：随着科技发展，电子屏在教学中广泛应用，有逐渐替代 传统黑板的趋势，但同时也出现了一些弊端。于是，是否要回归使 用传统黑板引起了广泛讨论。 Pro: Classrooms should use electronic screens. Con: Classrooms should stick to using traditional blackboards.

背景：在 AI 时代，信息获取便捷，知识更新迅速。有人认为 记忆背诵过时，但也有观点认为，它仍是学习基础，有助于知识内 化和思维发展。记忆背诵是否仍然重要在师生中引起了大讨论。 Pro: Memorization(记忆) is still important in the age of AI. Con: Memorization is no longer important in the age of AI.
背景：《西游记》中孙悟空最终成佛，引发了关于其个人成长 与自我认知的讨论。是成就了更高的自我，还是失去了原有的自我？ 这一问题值得深入探讨。 Pro: Wukong's Buddhahood(佛教；成为佛陀的状态) represents the achievement of self. Con: Wukong's Buddhahood represents the loss of self.

背景：“孟母三迁”讲述了孟母为给孟子提供良好成长环境三次 搬家的故事，引发了关于环境与个人努力在塑造人格中作用的讨论。 Pro: Environment（环境） is more important than individual （个人） effort in shaping a person's character. Con: Individual（个人） effort is more important than environment（环 境） in shaping a person's character.
